ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Most frequently mentioned and highly upvoted as a profoundly depressing film that leaves a lasting impact, specifically recommended for its anti-drug message.

ranked_movies.title: Grave of the Fireflies
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently brought up as a top choice for a movie that induces feelings of depression and sadness.

ranked_movies.title: The Mist
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited for its bleak and depressing ending that stays with viewers.

ranked_movies.title: The Road
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an extremely bleak and depressing post-apocalyptic film.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a prime example of a depressing movie and even listed as a 'comfort movie' for some despite its sadness.

ranked_movies.title: Old Yeller
ranked_movies.reason: A classic film remembered for its deeply sad and depressing ending.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as a disturbing and depressing film about youth.

ranked_movies.title: The Green Mile
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its emotional and depressing story, with the book noted as being even more so.

ranked_movies.title: Dear Zachary
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up as a documentary known to be incredibly sad and depressing.

ranked_movies.title: Million Dollar Baby
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a film with a very depressing storyline.

ranked_movies.title: Melancholia
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that deals with depression and apocalyptic themes.

ranked_movies.title: Boy in the Striped Pajamas
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its heartbreaking and depressing Holocaust narrative.

ranked_movies.title: Brokeback Mountain
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a tragic and depressing love story.

ranked_movies.title: Marley and Me
ranked_movies.reason: Anecdotally mentioned as a film that caused sadness, specifically for a viewer at age 16.

ranked_movies.title: The Pianist
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as another 'comfort movie' for a user who finds solace in depressing films.

ranked_movies.title: Lorenzo's Oil
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a dedicated post discussing its exploitative and unbearably depressing portrayal of a child's illness.

ranked_movies.title: Alien Resurrection
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed at length for its dystopian and depressing vision of the future within the Alien franchise.

ranked_movies.title: Friendship
ranked_movies.reason: The main topic of a post analyzing its tone, described as a dark comedy that leaves viewers feeling sad and confused rather than purely amused.

ranked_movies.title: The Master
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a post praising its portrayal of suicidal depression through the character Freddie Quell.

ranked_movies.title: Back to the Future Part II
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed not for being sad, but for bumming out a viewer whose hopeful vision of the future (2015) did not match the reality, making the film hard to watch.
